events.text: Patient is a 54-year-old Asian female with no smoking history (never smoker), ECOG performance status 0, BMI 23.5.

At age 54, patient was diagnosed with non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma (histology 8140), primary site right middle lobe (C34.2), clinical stage IVB, T2aN2M1c, with osseous and hepatic metastases.

At age 54, patient presented with right-sided rib pain and was found to have a pathologic fracture of the right 7th rib on imaging. Further workup revealed a lung mass with widespread metastatic disease. Notable for never-smoker status.

At age 54, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast showed a 2.8 x 2.5 cm mass in the right middle lobe with right hilar and bilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Multiple hepatic lesions in segments IV, VI, and VIII ranging from 0.8 to 2.4 cm. Lytic lesions in right 7th rib (with pathologic fracture), L2 vertebral body, and left iliac bone.

At age 54, PET/CT demonstrated FDG-avid right middle lobe mass (SUVmax 8.4), bilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy, multiple hepatic lesions (SUVmax 6.9), and osseous lesions in right 7th rib, L2 vertebra, and left iliac bone (SUVmax 11.2).
At age 54, MRI brain with and without contrast showed two small enhancing lesions in the left occipital lobe measuring 4 mm and 6 mm, consistent with brain metastases.

At age 54, CT-guided percutaneous biopsy of right middle lobe mass revealed well-differentiated adenocarcinoma with micropapillary features. Immunohistochemistry: TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A positive, CK7 positive, CK20 negative. PD-L1 TPS 0% (22C3 assay).

At age 54, next-generation sequencing (FoundationOne CDx) of tumor specimen: EGFR wild-type (no mutations in exons 18-21), ALK rearrangement not detected (confirmed by FISH), ROS1 rearrangement not detected, BRAF V600E not detected, KRAS wild-type, MET exon 14 skipping not detected, RET fusion not detected, NTRK fusion not detected, HER2 mutation not detected. TP53 R273H detected. No actionable driver mutations. Tumor mutational burden 3.1 mut/Mb. Microsatellite stable.

At age 54, multidisciplinary tumor board: stage IVB adenocarcinoma in a never-smoker with surprisingly no actionable driver mutations. PD-L1 negative. Low TMB. Recommended carboplatin/pemetrexed/pembrolizumab as standard first-line, though prognosis for immunotherapy response may be guarded given low PD-L1 and low TMB.
At age 54, received palliative radiation therapy to right 7th rib pathologic fracture site, 30 Gy in 10 fractions.
At age 54, received stereotactic radiosurgery to two left occipital lobe brain metastases, 20 Gy in 1 fraction each.
At age 54, initiated carboplatin AUC 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks. Also started denosumab 120 mg subcutaneous every 4 weeks for osseous metastatic disease.
At age 54, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 1 fatigue. Antiemetic regimen adjusted with addition of olanzapine.
At age 54, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 anemia (hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL).
At age 54, restaging CT after 4 cycles showed stable disease. Right middle lobe mass 2.6 x 2.3 cm (minor decrease). Hepatic lesions stable. No new osseous lesions.
At age 54, MRI brain showed stable treated occipital lesions with decreased enhancement. No new intracranial disease.
At age 54, completed 4 cycles of induction. Transitioned to pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 plus pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks maintenance.

At age 55, restaging CT after 4 cycles of maintenance showed progressive disease. Right middle lobe mass increased to 3.4 x 3.0 cm. New hepatic lesions identified. Segment VII 1.5 cm, segment III 1.1 cm.

At age 55, progressive disease on first-line pemetrexed/pembrolizumab maintenance. Given PD-L1 negative status and low TMB, immunotherapy resistance expected. Recommended second-line docetaxel plus ramucirumab.
At age 55, initiated docetaxel 75 mg/m2 plus ramucirumab 10 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks as second-line therapy.
At age 55, after cycle 2 of docetaxel/ramucirumab, patient developed grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, and grade 1 epistaxis. No hypertension noted.
At age 55, restaging CT after 4 cycles of docetaxel/ramucirumab showed partial response with right middle lobe mass decreased to 2.5 x 2.2 cm and hepatic lesions stable to slightly decreased.
At age 55, responding to second-line docetaxel/ramucirumab. Plan to continue therapy with close monitoring. Patient coping well psychologically with disease and treatment.

scenario_blurb: Stage IIIA-IV non-small cell lung cancer without actionable driver mutations (EGFR wild-type, ALK negative, ROS1 negative). Treated with carboplatin/pemetrexed plus pembrolizumab immunotherapy. Mix of adenocarcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma. Some with high PD-L1 expression receiving pembrolizumab monotherapy.
